track_prompt: Use the experiment-loop skill. Create a polished, reusable SVG event-card design system for a fictional "Loop Lab" workshop. The cards must communicate event title, date, time, venue, and call to action at a glance while remaining reusable across event variants. Score visual hierarchy, brand distinctiveness, information clarity, system coherence, production polish, SVG validity, and layout quality. Subjective criteria use a 1-5 point scale and maximize. SVG validity and layout quality use pass/fail objective gates: overlap, clipping, unreadable text, inaccessible contrast, missing required content, or invalid SVG prevents Champion promotion. Visual hierarchy is the primary criterion. [Editorial-typography Track scope: use claude-sonnet-5, at least two Loops, strong typographic rhythm, restrained geometric accents, clear reading order, print-grade spacing.]
input_feedback: Loop-01 established the grid, type scale, and component language and passed 4 of 6 objective gates (svg_parses, no_overlap, no_clipping, required_content_present) but failed 2 (readable_text, accessible_contrast), both caused by a single root cause: the ink.muted color token (#A39C8C) measured 2.49:1 against the paper background, well under the 4.5:1 AA threshold, across five text nodes (3 metadata labels, edition tag, microcopy). Loop-01's self-review also flagged that tokens.json described only one hardcoded event, with no evidence the grid generalizes.
judge_feedback: Self-measured via layout-metrics.json against the loop-01 baseline. Repairing ink.muted to #6B6558 (5.28:1) fixes all five affected text nodes with zero layout changes, since every shared coordinate (title, rule lines, column x-positions, CTA button, brand mark) is byte-for-byte identical to loop-01 -- confirmed by re-running the same overlap/clipping checks, which all still pass. The new format-badge component was independently contrast-checked (white on accent = 5.03:1, pass) and overlap/clipping-checked against its 3 nearest neighbors (edition tag, header rule, title) before being accepted, rather than assumed safe. All 6 objective gates now pass: svg_parses, no_overlap, no_clipping, readable_text, accessible_contrast, required_content_present. The eventVariants section in tokens.json now documents both the loop-01 in-person configuration and this loop's hybrid configuration on the identical grid, demonstrating the system generalizes rather than asserting it.
next_prompt: The editorial track has now demonstrated one full repair cycle (contrast defect found and fixed) and one variant (in-person -> hybrid with an optional format-badge component). A plausible next loop would stress-test a third variant with a longer venue/title string to find the grid's real capacity limit (current title budget is roughly 18-20 characters per line and venue budget roughly 320px/16 characters before column-width or line-count adjustments are needed), and would consider whether the format-badge should have a defined position rule when an event has both a long title and a badge simultaneously, since that combination has not yet been tested.
